features.title: Drive-leg planner
features.what: The Itinerary view now shows a per-day drive line under each day header — 🚐 distance (km) · drive time · estimated fuel cost · tolls/vignettes where relevant — plus a whole-loop rollup footer (total km, total fuel €, total tolls €). Days where the van stays on the same base are clearly flagged 🅿️ 'van stays put · no drive today'. Numbers are sourced from existing data, no new deps: distance is the road-scaled haversine (×1.3) between consecutive day bases (same model the budget fuel table uses), drive time is each day's authored `drive` hours, fuel uses meta.fuelPerH as litres/100km × the €1.80/L price, and tolls come from the per-day budget.x figures. A pure drivePlan() helper in core.js carries the logic. Frontend-only.
features.verify_note: Green-gate PASS — node --check js/core.js js/itinerary.js OK, alpine.json parses, tools/test-core.mjs exits 0, local served-check 200 + 'Travel Companion' marker. Playwright re-drive of #alpine/itinerary: ok=true, pageErrors=[]; eval confirms 16 drive legs rendered, 2 'van stays put' flags, 1 rollup showing 1601 km · fuel €375 · tolls/vignettes €151, sample leg '🚐 317 km · ~4h · fuel €74 · tolls/vignettes €40'. Commit 1cf6749 pushed to main.

features.title: One-tap “Open full route in Google Maps”
features.what: The Overview tab now has an “Open full route in Google Maps” button that launches Google Maps with the entire trip as one multi-stop driving route — origin → every overnight base in order → final destination — so you can hand the whole loop to the Maps app in a single tap. It builds a standard /maps/dir/ directions deep link from each day's coordinates (no API key, no network needed to construct it), and consecutive nights at the same base collapse into one stop so the route stays clean. On the seeded Alpine grand loop that's a 19-stop driving itinerary. The link opens the native Maps app on a phone. A pure gmapsRouteUrl(days) helper in core.js carries the logic (and hides the button when there aren't two distinct stops to route between), guarded by tests. Frontend-only, no backend, no new deps.
features.verify_note: Independent agent PASS — artifacts on origin/main 9b24880 (gmapsRouteUrl in js/core.js, “Open full route in Google Maps” link in js/overview.js); node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 5 B36 assertions; function fed real alpine.json yields a /maps/dir/ URL of 19 ordered lat,lng stops (consecutive dup collapsed); local + live GitHub Pages served-checks 200 with the feature markers.

features.title: Dark mode (light / dark / auto theme toggle)
features.what: A theme toggle now sits in the header (🌗 Auto / ☀️ Light / 🌙 Dark) — tap to cycle and your choice sticks across reloads via localStorage. Dark mode restyles the whole app chrome and cards through a single dark palette (only the CSS colour variables change), while map tiles are left untouched so they stay legible. 'Auto' follows your phone's OS preference live, flipping with the system as it changes. To avoid a flash of the wrong theme, the saved choice is applied by a tiny inline script before first paint. Two pure helpers in core.js — cycleTheme() (auto→light→dark→auto) and effectiveTheme(choice, prefersDark) — are guarded by tests. Frontend-only, no backend, no new deps.
features.verify_note: Independent agent PASS — artifacts on origin/main 3522d11 (cycleTheme/effectiveTheme in core.js, #themetoggle + v2:theme early script in index.html, html[data-theme=dark] in app.css); node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 8 B35 assertions; live Pages served-checks 200 with markers effectiveTheme / themetoggle / data-theme.

features.title: "Open now" badges on itinerary places
features.what: Itinerary places that carry opening-hours from live enrichment now show a colour-coded Open now / Closed badge right next to the name — and when a spot is shut but opens later today, the badge tells you when ("Closed · opens 9:00 AM"). Crucially the badge is computed for the current time on your phone, not a stale cached snapshot: a new pure openStatus()/parseDayHours() in core.js parses today's hours string (handling "Mon: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM", split lunch breaks like "9 AM – 12 PM, 1 – 6 PM", "Open 24 hours", "Closed", and overnight ranges that cross midnight) into minute-of-day ranges and checks the clock. Places with no/unknown hours show nothing at all — no false "closed" state. Frontend-only, no backend change, covered by guarded tests.
features.verify_note: Independent agent: origin/main merge 0388334 has openStatus()/parseDayHours() in js/core.js and the .pbadge span + open-now/closed-now classes in itinerary.js + app.css. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with all new B34 guards ok. Sanity: openStatus('9:00 AM – 6:00 PM',600)=open, at 480=Closed·opens 9:00 AM, null hours→null (no false state), parseDayHours('Sunday: Closed')=[]. Live GitHub Pages served-checks 200 with markers 'openStatus' (core.js) and 'pbadge' (itinerary.js).

features.title: Free-form day notes
features.what: Every day card in the Itinerary now has a plain-text notes box so you can jot day-specific reminders right where you'll see them — "fill water at the aire before the pass", "ZTL zone, don't drive into the centre", "market closes 13:00". Type a note, tap away, and it's saved instantly to the trip's itinerary overlay (the same place your re-ordered plans, modes and per-place notes live), so it survives a reload and works fully offline. Leaving the box empty clears the note. A new pure dayNote(overlay, dayId) helper in core.js reads it back trimmed (degrading to empty for missing/blank), seeding the textarea on every render so the round-trip is genuine, and is covered by guarded tests.
features.verify_note: Independent agent: origin/main merge 1dbf735 has dayNote() in core.js and data-daynote textarea + setDayNote (writes ov.dayNotes via ctx.save) in itinerary.js; render seeds the box from dayNote(state.overlay.itinerary, day.id) so it survives reload. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with all 5 new dayNote guards ok. Live GitHub Pages served-checks 200 with markers 'data-daynote' (itinerary.js) and 'function dayNote' (core.js).

features.title: Daylight per day
features.what: Each day in the Itinerary now shows that day's sunrise and sunset alongside the weather chip (e.g. "🌅 05:38 🌇 21:02"), with a golden-hour hint and total daylight length in the tooltip — handy for planning drives and photos on the campervan loop. The times come from the open-meteo daily endpoint that weather.js already fetches (now also requesting sunrise/sunset), so there's no extra network call and they're cached per coordinate for offline use: with no signal the last-known forecast still renders the daylight, and if a stale cache predates this feature (no sun fields) the chip simply hides rather than showing anything wrong. A new pure daylight() helper in core.js parses the ISO sunrise/sunset, computes the day length and the evening golden hour (~1h before sunset), and is covered by guarded tests.
features.verify_note: Independent agent: origin/main d6118cb has daylight() + sunrise,sunset in core.js weatherUrl/pickDaily, imported+used in itinerary.js .dl chip. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with all 4 daylight assertions ok (incl. null-degrade when sun fields absent). Logic confirmed: 2026-06-15 → rise 05:38/set 21:02/15h 24m/golden 20:02. Live GitHub Pages core.js served-check 200 with 'function daylight' marker after propagation.

features.title: Trip totals + departure countdown
features.what: The Overview tab now opens with a live departure countdown ("X days to departure" from today to the trip start — and "Today the trip begins" / "trip under way" once it starts) plus a compact stats strip summarising the whole trip at a glance: total driving distance (road-scaled km across the day bases), total driving time (the sum of each day's drive hours), number of nights, planned stops and bookings, and the day count. Two new pure functions in core.js — tripTotals() and daysToDeparture() — power it; both read only already-loaded data so it works fully offline. The stops figure is overlay-aware (your re-ordered/edited day plans are counted), and the countdown is UTC-anchored so it never drifts by a day across timezones.
features.verify_note: Independent agent: origin/main 4d49f62 has tripTotals + daysToDeparture in core.js, imported+used in overview.js (ov-count countdown + ov-stats grid). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with all 15 B31 assertions ok; logic confirmed correct (driveHours sums day.drive, nights counts sleep bases, stops overlay-aware, km via routeStats, countdown UTC-anchored). Live GitHub Pages served-checks 200 with markers present after propagation.

features.title: Campervan fuel cost estimator
features.what: The Budget view now has a Fuel estimate card for campervan trips. It breaks the route into driving legs between consecutive day bases, measures each leg with the road-scaled distance (haversine ×1.3) between the day coordinates, and estimates litres from the van's consumption figure (meta.fuelPerH, treated as L/100km) and cost from a price/litre you can edit inline — the total and every per-leg figure update live as you change the price (default €1.80/L, remembered in the expenses overlay). Same-base nights (zero-distance legs) are dropped so only real drives show. A new pure fuelEstimate() lives in core.js with a guarded test; the card only appears when the trip models fuel use (so the no-driving Iceland trip is unaffected).
features.verify_note: Independent agent: origin/main 6874fe9 has fuelEstimate in core.js, imported+used in budget.js, guarded B30 test in test-core. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with all 6 B30 assertions ok; sample run confirms sensible legs/totals, price 0 → cost 0, doubling price doubles total. All served-checks 200 incl. live GitHub Pages core.js with fuelEstimate marker.

features.title: Satellite view + remembered map layer
features.what: On the Google Maps backend the Map view now defaults to a satellite layer (best for spotting campervan scenery and terrain) and lets you switch between roadmap / satellite / terrain / hybrid using the built-in map-type control. Whichever layer you pick is remembered in localStorage (key v2:mapType) via a maptypeid_changed listener, so it sticks across reloads instead of snapping back. A new pure mapTypeChoice() in core.js normalises the stored value and falls back to satellite for anything missing or unknown. The Leaflet/OpenStreetMap fallback (used when no key or offline) is untouched — no map-type code, no error.
features.verify_note: Independent agent: origin/main c2125a6 has mapTypeId:mapTypeChoice(stored), v2:mapType read/write + maptypeid_changed listener; core.js mapTypeChoice defaults to satellite; renderLeaflet unchanged. test-core exits 0 (8 new mapTypeChoice tests pass); all 3 live served-checks 200 with markers present.

features.title: Campervan pitch-to-book nights
features.what: On a campervan trip the trip-spanning van rental used to count as the bed for every night, so 'Where you sleep' read 16/16 covered and no night was tappable to book a pitch — a false green hiding the planner's real job (book a Stellplatz each night). The sleep strip now flags van-only nights as pitch-pending, renders them as tappable 'pitch to book' cells (amber, dashed) that pre-fill the booking form, and reports 'X pitches booked · Y to book'. Additive pitch flag on accommodationStrip — covered/name unchanged so the camper-bed gap contract and core tests are untouched.
features.verify_note: Before: click [data-booknight=2026-08-05] timed out (no gap handle, all covered). After: same click succeeds + pre-fills form; drive ok==true, pageErrors==[]; test-core exits 0; served-check 200 marker present. Screenshot shows header '... pitches booked · ... to book' and amber pitch cells.

features.title: Trip overview timeline
features.what: A new "Overview" tab gives the whole trip at a glance: one compact row per day showing its date, the day's headline (or first planned stop), the first stop as a subline, and a marker icon for each booking that day — so you can see the entire trip without scrolling through the big day cards. Tap any day to jump straight to it in the Itinerary view (it scrolls that day's card into view). Read-only and fully offline: it works off already-loaded data via a new pure tripOverview() in core.js.
features.verify_note: Independent agent PASS: overview.js + app.js 'overview' view + core.js tripOverview + itinerary daycard id anchor all on origin/main (merge 1f6aad0); node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 8 new tripOverview assertions; local + GitHub Pages served checks 200 with markers (Travel Companion, tripOverview, overview) on first try.

features.title: Calendar export (.ics)
features.what: The Bookings view now has a "📅 Export trip calendar (.ics)" button that downloads the whole trip as one standard calendar file: one event per booking (flights, stays, transport, activities — with provider, confirmation #, price and location) plus one event per timed itinerary stop (start/end pulled from the stop's HH:MM time). Untimed stops and undated days are skipped, and times are floating-local (no timezone surprises), so the file drops cleanly into Apple Calendar, Google Calendar or Outlook. Built on a new pure tripIcs() that shares the same VEVENT builder as the existing single-booking add-to-calendar.
features.verify_note: Independent agent PASS: tripIcs + #bkics button on origin/main (merge 2061f72); node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 13 new tripIcs assertions; live throwaway import produced a valid VCALENDAR (2 bookings + 1 timed stop = 3 VEVENTs, untimed/undated skipped); live served checks 200 with markers on first try.

features.title: Map view can run on Google Maps
features.what: The Map tab now supports a real embedded Google Maps interface (Maps JavaScript API) with all the existing whole-trip / per-day / per-category views, coloured numbered pins, popups and Google/Apple deep links preserved. It activates the moment a Google Maps API key is set in js/config.js (referrer-restricted, safe to expose); until then it falls back to the current Leaflet/OpenStreetMap map so nothing breaks. Add the key (5-min setup, steps in config.js) to switch the interface to Google Maps.
features.verify_note: Built in main session (not the loop). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 (sw guard now includes js/config.js); node --check clean on map.js/config.js/sw.js; served checks 200 with markers (map.js 'renderGoogle', config.js 'MAPS_KEY'); empty key correctly selects the Leaflet fallback. Google path awaits the owner's API key for live visual confirmation.

features.title: Country essentials card
features.what: A new "Essentials" tab gives each trip an offline survival card for its destination country: the emergency number (front-and-centre, with separate police/ambulance lines), the local currency, the mains plug type + voltage, and a short list of language basics (hello, thank you, please, yes/no, excuse me, how much?, help!) with the local phrasing. It's pure static reference data baked into the app (no API), so it renders instantly with zero network and is precached by the service worker for full offline use in the field. Each trip now carries a country code (Alpine + Pre-exchange → Italy, Iceland → Iceland); the Alpine (Italy) trip shows emergency 112, Euro (EUR), and Type C/F/L plugs at 230V.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b0f1bbe on origin/main: js/essentials.js view wired into js/app.js VIEWS, countryEssentials()+COUNTRY_ESSENTIALS in js/core.js, "country":"IT" on the alpine trip in data/trips.json, and js/essentials.js added to the sw.js precache shell (CACHE bumped v6). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 8 new B14 guards (Italy 112/EUR/plug C-F, case-insensitive lookup, unknown/null→null, every trip resolves). Live GitHub Pages fetch of js/essentials.js returned HTTP 200 with the countryEssentials marker.

features.title: Weather-aware re-plan nudge on the Today view
features.what: When today is mostly outdoors and rain is in the forecast, the mobile Today view now slips a gentle blue banner above the day — "Rain's forecast and most of today is outdoors. Consider indoor swaps nearby — museums, galleries, cafés, thermal spas." It only fires when it should: the day must have at least two tagged stops and a strict majority of them outdoor (views, hikes, lakes, towns, swims…), and the forecast must show real precipitation (≥50% chance or a WMO rain/snow code), so a clear day, a foggy day, or a museum-heavy day stays silent — no nagging on clean data. It reuses the same cached open-meteo forecast the weather chip already loads, so it works offline from the last-known forecast, and the indoor suggestions echo the B07 nearby-discovery categories so the swap is one tab away.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b31f421 on origin/main (replanNudge exported from js/core.js, imported+used in js/today.js → #t-nudge). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 8 new replanNudge guards: rainy+mostly-outdoor→nudge, and null for clear day, fog (code 45), mostly-indoor, single-stop, and no-weather (no false positives). Live served checks: js/core.js + js/today.js HTTP 200 with replanNudge marker on GitHub Pages.

features.title: Quick currency converter in the Budget view
features.what: A small live converter sits in the Budget tab: type an amount in the trip currency (EUR) and instantly see it in your home currency — or type in the home currency and it back-converts to EUR. Pick the home currency from a dropdown of every rate fx.js knows (defaults to USD) and it sticks across reloads. It uses the same Frankfurter/ECB live rates the budget roll-up already pulls, so once you've opened the app online the rate is cached and the converter keeps working offline in the field. Typing updates the other field without a re-render, so focus and the caret never jump.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 0bd7adc on origin/main (fxConvert exported from js/core.js; "Currency converter" budcard in js/budget.js). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 8 new fxConvert guards incl. both-direction round-trip (100 EUR @1.08 → 108 USD; 108 → 100), 2dp rounding, and null/NaN/zero-rate→null. Live served checks: js/budget.js + js/core.js HTTP 200 with markers on GitHub Pages.

features.title: Offline trip search — instantly find any place, note or booking
features.what: A new “Search” tab puts a single box across the whole open trip: type and it filters live across every place in your plan (name, description, time), every day note/tip, and every booking (title, provider, confirmation #, location). Matching is multi-token AND and case-insensitive — “camping peschiera” narrows to that one stay; results are grouped by Places / Notes / Bookings with a match count. Clearing the box restores the full picture. Critically it runs entirely on already-loaded data with zero network calls, so it works in the field with no signal — exactly when you're scrambling to remember which night the campervan covers or what the hotel confirmation number was.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b58f0fd on origin/main (searchRecords exported from js/core.js; new js/search.js view registered in js/app.js VIEWS/labels). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 9 new searchRecords guards (multi-token AND within a record, no cross-record match, case-insensitive, empty/whitespace/null→[], booking conf #, no-match→[]). Grep of js/search.js found NO fetch/XHR/WebSocket — operates only on ctx.state. Live served checks: js/search.js + js/app.js HTTP 200 with markers; GitHub Pages js/search.js 200 on first try.

features.title: Next-up countdown on the Today view — live time to your next stop & booking
features.what: The Today view now has a live “Next up” section that counts down to whatever's coming, so a glance at your phone tells you how long you've got. It shows the soonest still-upcoming timed stop in today's plan (“📍 Next stop · Funicular — 1h 20m”) and the next upcoming reservation (“🛏 Next booking · Hotel Bellagio — 5h 50m”), each as its own chip with a tabular countdown. The numbers refresh every minute on their own, and the section degrades gracefully — once nothing is left it simply reads “Nothing left scheduled today.” The countdown only appears when the open day really is today, so previewing a future or past day stays clean. All computed from already-loaded data, so it works fully offline.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b5b0189 on origin/main (nextUpcoming + fmtCountdown in js/core.js, the 'Next up' section + 60s setInterval ticker in js/today.js). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with new guards: soonest future stop, minutes-until, next/next-day booking roll, null-when-none, and fmtCountdown 80→'1h 20m'/45→'45m'/120→'2h'/0→'now'. Live served checks 200 with markers 'Next up' (today.js) + nextUpcoming (core.js). Sanity import: stop {mins:80} + booking selected, past items excluded.

features.title: Booking detail drawer — tap a booking for the full picture + add-to-calendar
features.what: Every booking card in the Bookings view now has a tappable “Details” drawer that expands to show the full record at a glance — type, provider, exact start/end times, location, travellers, price and any notes — laid out as a clean field list. The confirmation number gets a one-tap Copy button (great when an airline or hotel app asks you to paste it), the location gets a direct Map link (Google Maps coords when known, otherwise a name search), and there's an 📅 Add to calendar button that downloads a single-booking .ics file you can open in Apple/Google/Outlook calendar. The .ics is generated locally with no backend: timed bookings export with floating local DTSTART/DTEND, a timed booking with no end gets a sensible 1-hour block, and a date-only booking becomes an all-day event — with commas/special characters properly escaped per the iCalendar spec.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge dec79aa on origin/main (bookingIcs in js/core.js, detailHtml + data-ics + data-copyconf in js/bookings.js, .bkdetail/.bkmini in css/app.css). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with new guards: VCALENDAR/VEVENT wrap, floating-local DTSTART/DTEND, UID from id, DTSTAMP passthrough, comma+LOCATION escaping, conf in DESCRIPTION, date-only→all-day, timed-no-end→PT1H. Live served checks 200 with markers bookingIcs (bookings.js) + bkdetail (app.css).

features.title: Transport continuity check — does your travel chain actually connect?
features.what: The Bookings view now runs a 🔗 Transport continuity check across your flights, trains, buses and ferries. It parses each leg's origin→destination from the booking title (airport IATA codes for flights, station/city names for ground transit) and flags three kinds of problem: a same-time jump (two timed legs overlap but depart different places — you can't be in two places at once), a broken connection (you land somewhere but the next same-day leg departs a different place), and a one-way rental (a car picked up in one city but dropped in another, with no return to where you started). It's deliberately conservative: legs days apart aren't flagged (an unbooked local transfer is normal), round-trip “↔” car rentals are treated as self-returning, and city matching is lenient on station detail — so a clean multi-city itinerary like the seeded Alpine/Iceland trip raises nothing, while a deliberately broken chain surfaces immediately.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 75e8e3d on origin/main (transportContinuity in js/core.js, continuityHtml in js/bookings.js, .bk-cont styles in css/app.css). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with new guards: clean chain + round-trip van → none, same-day arrive≠depart → break, overlapping legs → jump, one-way car → noreturn, legs days apart → no false break. Independent module-mode exercise reproduced each verdict and confirmed seeded data/bookings.json raises [] (no false positives). Live served checks 200 with markers transportContinuity (core.js) + continuityHtml (bookings.js).

features.title: Committed so far — booking spend rolled up by type vs the trip budget
features.what: The Bookings view now shows a 💶 Committed so far card that totals every priced reservation for the trip and breaks it down by headline type — Flights, Stays, Transport (train/bus/car/ferry), Activities and Other — each with a proportional bar. Foreign-currency bookings are FX-converted into the trip's base currency using the same live-rate path the Budget view uses (cached for the day, graceful fallback offline), so the grand total is apples-to-apples. The card also reads the trip's guide budget via a shared tripEstimate helper (now the single source of that figure for both Budget and Bookings) and shows what fraction is already reserved — e.g. “42% of the €1,200 trip budget already reserved.” Bookings with no price are ignored, so an all-unpriced trip shows nothing. On the seeded Alpine trip the rollup sums the reservations by type with a correct grand total and ties into the existing budget number.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge baf738f on origin/main (bookingRollup + tripEstimate in js/core.js, rollupHtml 💶 Committed so far card in js/bookings.js, budget.js refactored to use tripEstimate, .bk-rollup styles in css/app.css). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with new guards: grand total sums priced-only, count, group mapping (train+car→transport, fixed order), FX-convert via toBase, and tripEstimate bu/sp/no-days. Live served checks 200 with markers bookingRollup (core.js) + rollupHtml (bookings.js).

features.title: Needs attention — time-sensitive booking reminders sorted by urgency
features.what: The Bookings view now leads with a ⏰ Needs attention list that surfaces the booking actions with a deadline, soonest first. It derives a flight's online check-in window straight from departure time (opens ~48h before, closes ~1.5h before) and only shows it once it's actually relevant — already open (flagged urgent), or opening within the next few days — so it never nags you to check in weeks early. It reads a booking's free-cancellation deadline (free_cancellation_until) and a hotel's check-in/check-out times (checkin_time/checkout_time), showing each only while it's still in the future. Anything due within 24h is highlighted urgent. A booking missing the relevant field raises nothing, so there's no spurious noise. The Alpine trip's Rialto Venice stay now carries real check-in (15:00), check-out (11:00) and free-cancellation (13 Aug) fields, so the list shows them in order.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge e1ae08d on origin/main (bookingReminders in js/core.js, remindersHtml ⏰ Needs attention block in js/bookings.js, .bk-remind styles in css/app.css, additive checkin_time/checkout_time/free_cancellation_until on the Rialto booking). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 7 new bookingReminders guards (sorted-ascending by due, open check-in urgent, beyond-horizon suppressed, no-field hotel raises nothing, bad/empty input → []). Independent throwaway run reproduced correct sort + urgency + no-spurious behaviour. Live served checks 200 with markers bookingReminders + Needs attention.

features.title: Where you sleep — a per-night strip showing which nights are booked and which are gaps
features.what: The Bookings view now opens with a 🛏 coverage strip — one cell per night of the trip (the final departure day is excluded since it needs no bed). Each night is colour-coded: a green cell with the hotel/stay name when a booking spans that night (check-in ≤ night < checkout, so checkout day doesn't double-count), or a dashed terracotta gap when nothing covers it. The header counts covered nights at a glance (e.g. “6/7 nights covered”). A campervan/motorhome counts as the bed it is, matching the “Still to book” logic so the two never disagree. Tapping an uncovered night jumps straight to the manual add form pre-filled as a hotel for that date (check-in 15:00, checkout next morning) so you can lock the stay in one tap. On the seeded Alpine trip every booked night shows covered with its stay name and any uncovered night shows a clear, tappable gap.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 568f4b2 on origin/main (accommodationStrip in js/core.js, stripHtml + data-booknight quick-add in js/bookings.js, .bk-night/.bk-strip in css/app.css). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 9 new accommodationStrip guards ok (per-night cells, departure-day excluded, checkout-exclusive coverage, campervan-as-bed, uncovered→false, empty edge cases). Live served checks 200 with markers Travel Companion + accommodationStrip + bk-strip.

features.title: Booking timeline — every reservation in one chronological, day-by-day view
features.what: A new ⏱ Timeline tab gives every booking for the trip its own dedicated, read-only chronological view: grouped by day and sorted by start time, each row shows the type icon, the time (start–end), provider, confirmation # and price. Time conflicts are surfaced two ways — a header banner counts how many bookings overlap, and any clashing entry is highlighted inline with an “overlap” tag — so a double-booked window jumps out at a glance. It reuses the same overlap detection as the Bookings warnings, so flags stay consistent across the app. On the seeded Alpine data the reservations render in time order under each day with no false conflicts; a deliberately overlapping pair flags both entries.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 3041b71 on origin/main (bookingTimeline in js/core.js, new js/timeline.js, timeline view registered in js/app.js, precached in sw.js). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 6 new bookingTimeline guards (date-sort, within-day time-sort, overlap flagging, empty/undated edge cases). A throwaway import fed two overlapping flights → both overlap:true. Local + live served checks 200 with marker 'Booking timeline'.

features.title: “Still to book” — auto-spots the nights and travel legs you haven't booked
features.what: The Bookings view now shows a 🧳 “Still to book” checklist that pressure-tests your reservations against your itinerary. It flags two kinds of gap: any night with no accommodation booked, and any move where your overnight base changes (the day's “sleep” location differs) but no transport covers the hop. It's smart about road trips — a campervan/motorhome counts as the bed while you've got it, and any vehicle you're holding covers every driving leg, so a van trip on a single rental booking raises nothing. Gaps are grouped by date so you can see at a glance what's left to lock in. On the seeded pre-exchange trip it correctly lists the un-booked Genova→Firenze and Firenze→Bologna legs while showing zero false alarms on the fully-booked hotel nights; the Alpine campervan loop shows clean.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge f06ba5c on origin/main (coverageGaps in js/core.js, stillToBookHtml in js/bookings.js, .bk-tobook in css/app.css). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 6 new coverageGaps guards ok. On real seeded data: pre-exchange flags exactly the missing transport legs with 0 false lodging gaps; the Alpine trip-spanning campervan suppresses all ~15 base changes (only the genuine post-return tail remains). Live served checks 200 with markers coverageGaps + stillToBookHtml.

features.title: Add a booking by hand — provider, end time, location & travellers
features.what: The Bookings view's “＋ Add a booking manually” form now captures a full reservation, not just the basics. Alongside type, title, start time, confirmation # and price, you can now add the provider (e.g. Booking.com), an optional end time, the location, and the travellers on the booking (comma-separated). Whatever you enter is trimmed, auto-filed to the right trip by its start date, and saved into your local bookings overlay's manual list — so a hand-added hotel shows up in the timeline on its own day exactly like a booking imported from Gmail, and survives a reload. Empty optional fields are dropped cleanly so cards stay tidy, and a typed location with no map pin now shows as a 📍 line on the card.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge cb5e914 on origin/main: new pure buildManualBooking() in js/core.js trims fields, files via assignTrip, splits pax on commas, defaults currency, stamps source:'manual'; js/bookings.js form adds provider/end/location/pax inputs and the submit handler routes through buildManualBooking into the manual[] overlay (ctx.save). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 4 new buildManualBooking guards ok (full hotel→filed to alpine, bare→nulls, blank/defaulted price). LIVE served checks 200 with marker buildManualBooking in js/bookings.js and js/core.js (no propagation lag).

features.title: Suggest a packing list from the weather + your plan
features.what: The Checklists view has a new “🎒 Suggest packing list” button that builds a packing checklist tailored to your actual trip — no guessing, no generic template. It reads each day's weather forecast (the same open-meteo data the Today view uses, cached so it still works offline) and scans your day plans, then assembles a deduped list: rain shell + waterproof shoes if any day shows rain, a warm fleece/down layer for cold alpine lows, hat & gloves and thermal base layer when it's near freezing, sun hat & sunscreen for hot or sunny days, plus activity-aware extras — hiking boots & a daypack if your plan mentions a funicular, summit or mountain trail, and swimwear & a quick-dry towel if there's a lake or swim. Base travel essentials (passport, charger, power bank, meds, water bottle, walking shoes) are always included, with laundry sheets for trips of five days or more. One tap drops it in as a normal checklist you can tick off.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 64516b2 on origin/main: pure suggestPacking(dayInfos) in js/core.js derives items from per-day weather + plan text, '🎒 Suggest packing list' button in js/checklists.js gathers weather via weather.js and adds a 'Packing (suggested)' checklist. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with all 10 new suggestPacking guards ok (rain→shell, alpine lows→warm layer, funicular→hiking boots, lake→swimwear, base essentials always, no dups). Local + LIVE served checks 200 with marker 'Suggest packing list' in js/checklists.js (live propagated on retry).

features.title: Today view shows where you are in the day
features.what: On the day of travel, the Today view no longer shows a flat list where every stop looks the same. Each timed stop is now marked by where you are in the day: the stop you're in right now is highlighted with a gold border and a NOW pill, your next stop carries a NEXT pill, and stops you've already finished fade out with a strike-through. A stop with only a start time counts as ‘now’ until the next one begins. At a glance — without reading every time — you can see what's happening now and what's coming up. This only kicks in on the real current day; previewing a future or past day shows the plain plan.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge dc13fc2 on origin/main: pure planProgress(plan, nowHHMM) in js/core.js (now/next/past/'' per stop), today.js imports it and renders NOW/NEXT pills + highlight gated on rel==='today', new B11 guards in tools/test-core.mjs. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 (7 planProgress assertions ok); agent's own independent logic assertions passed. Local + LIVE served checks 200 with markers (core.js 'function planProgress', today.js 't-pill-now'); live propagated on first attempt.

features.title: Print / share — day-by-day itinerary handout
features.what: A new “🖨 Print day-by-day” button at the top of the Itinerary view turns your trip into a clean paper backup. It opens your browser's print/save dialog with a dedicated print stylesheet: all the interactive chrome — the trip switcher, view tabs, add-a-place box, suggestion chips, drag handles, detail drawers and action buttons — drops away, and each day breaks onto its own page. What stays is exactly what you'd want on a printed handout or saved PDF: the day number and date, the day's places in order with their times and notes, the route summary, and that day's bookings. Print to paper or “Save as PDF” for an offline backup that works with zero signal.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b5f243e on origin/main: @media print block in css/app.css (hides #viewtabs/.addplace/.suggs/.grab/.dayactions via display:none, forces per-day .daycard+.daycard break-before:page, keeps .planlist/.bkchip/.dayhead) and window.print() wired to the #itprint button in js/itinerary.js. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0. Local + LIVE served checks of css/app.css (marker '@media print') and js/itinerary.js (marker 'window.print') all HTTP 200; live already propagated.

features.title: Forward-to address label in bookings
features.what: The bookings view now shows a clear, dedicated line — “📨 Forward booking confirmations to wchongyu2001@gmail.com” — with a one-tap Copy button, so you always know exactly where to forward a flight/hotel/train confirmation. Forwarded mail flows in through the existing daily pipeline sync and lands in this timeline automatically; no app setup needed. The address lives in a single INBOUND_ADDR constant, ready to swap to a dedicated inbound-parse address later.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge c8c0c83 on origin/main: INBOUND_ADDR + .bk-forward-addr/#bkcopyaddr in js/bookings.js and .bk-forward* rules in css/app.css. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 (no failures). Live homepage HTTP 200 (marker Travel Companion) and live js/bookings.js HTTP 200 with the bk-forward-addr marker after Pages propagation.

features.title: Faster expense entry + budget vs actual per day
features.what: Logging a spend is now near-instant: the quick-add form needs only an amount — the note is optional and category, who-paid, and 50/50-vs-payer-only split all default sensibly, so it's type-the-number-and-Add (≤3 taps). Underneath, the settle-up summary still spells out exactly who owes whom. And a new "Budget vs actual" card breaks the trip down day by day — each day's guide estimate next to what you've actually logged, with the difference coloured (terra = over, pine = under) and a running total at the top — so you can see at a glance which Alpine days ran hot or came in under.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 1cf8e9f on origin/main: new pure budgetVsActual(days,expenses) in core.js + Budget-vs-actual card wired in budget.js; the amount input is the only required field (title now optional). test-core exits 0 with 4 new budgetVsActual guards; pure-logic sanity check returned actual 120/delta 20/totals delta 20 as expected. Live core.js & budget.js served HTTP 200 with the budgetVsActual marker on first try (no Pages lag).

features.title: Nearby discovery into itinerary
features.what: Open any place in the itinerary and a new "Nearby eat & do" panel now suggests real spots around it — restaurants, cafés, bars, gelaterias, viewpoints, museums and attractions — pulled live from OpenStreetMap (free, no API key, cached for offline). They're ranked nearest-first, and one tap on a chip drops it straight into that day's plan with its category as a note. So when you're standing in an Alpine town wondering where to eat or what's worth a look, the options are one tap away instead of a separate map search.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b19bf3d on origin/main (pure overpassUrl/parseOverpass/nearbyCacheKey in core.js; .pd-nearby drawer + data-near chips pushing via setPlan in itinerary.js); test-core exits 0 with 7 new B07 guards (named-only, deduped, nearest-first, amenity→food/tourism→act mapping); live core.js & itinerary.js serve HTTP 200 with the parseOverpass and pd-nearby markers after Pages propagation.

features.title: "Can I make it?" timing warnings
features.what: Your itinerary now sanity-checks the clock. Between consecutive stops, the travel-time connector compares the real driving/walking time against the gap you've actually scheduled — if the hop won't fit, it turns red and warns "⚠ tight by N min" so you know before you're standing there late. Whole days get checked too: if your stops plus travel blow past a sensible waking budget, a "⚠ Packed day" banner appears. On the seeded Alpine trip, the zero-buffer Sirmione → Jamaica Beach hop flags as tight while the relaxed afternoon stops stay quiet.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge f0bff42 on origin/main (pure legGapMins/legFeasibility/dayLoad in core.js, leg-tight + packed-day banner in itinerary.js); test-core exits 0 with new B06 guards; an independent import check confirmed a tight leg (90m travel vs 30m gap) → {tight:true} and a comfortable leg → {tight:false}; live core.js & itinerary.js serve HTTP 200 with the feature markers.

features.title: Unassigned-booking triage
features.what: No booking can silently fall through the cracks anymore. The 📥 Unassigned inbox in the bookings view now catches every booking that belongs to no trip — ones with no trip set, ones tagged 'unassigned', and ones still pointing at a trip you've since removed. Each gets a clear "→ assign to…" dropdown; pick a trip and it's filed instantly and remembered (saved to your bookings overlay).
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge bb862dc on origin/main (new pure orphanBookings in core.js, wired into bookings.js inbox); test-core exits 0 with 2 new B05 guards (flags unassigned/no-trip/unknown-trip, clean booking → none); live js/core.js serves HTTP 200 with the orphanBookings marker.

features.title: Booking gap & conflict detector
features.what: The bookings view now scans your trip and flags problems in a dismissible banner: bookings dated outside the trip, two bookings overlapping the same time window, and broken flight chains (you arrive at one airport but the next flight departs another — a missing connecting/return leg). Conservative by design — hotels and campervans never count as time clashes and clean multi-city itineraries stay silent, so the seeded Alpine & Iceland trips raise nothing.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 66f686f on origin/main (bookingWarnings in core.js, bk-warnings banner in bookings.js); test-core exits 0 with 6 new B04 guards; detector raises 0 warnings on real preexchange/alpine/iceland data and 1 on a deliberately broken booking; live core.js serves 200 with the marker.

features.title: Mobile "Today" view
features.what: A single, thumb-friendly screen for the road: today's date and weather, the day's ordered plan with times, and the next upcoming booking. It auto-opens when the trip's dates contain today, and is always reachable from the new Today tab (previewing day 1 before the trip starts).
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed: js/today.js + 'today' view registration + auto-select on origin/main, sw.js precaches it; test-core exits 0 with new pickTodayDay/nextBooking guards; live js/today.js serves HTTP 200 with the Today-view marker.

features.title: Offline map tiles + last-known weather
features.what: Map tiles you've already viewed are kept in a dedicated cache (surviving app updates) and re-served with no signal — blank gaps instead of broken images where uncached. The day forecast is persisted per location, so weather still renders offline from the last-known copy.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed: tc-tiles tile cache + OSM handling + weatherCacheKey present on origin/main; test-core exits 0 with new B02 guards; live sw.js serves HTTP 200 with the tc-tiles marker.

features.title: Offline PWA shell
features.what: Service worker + manifest cache the whole app shell and every trip's JSON, so the Alpine itinerary, map and Today view open with no signal.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed live: homepage, sw.js and manifest all serve HTTP 200; service worker registered; test-core green with new precache guards.

features.title: Optimize day order
features.what: The per-day ⚡ optimize button now reorders stops nearest-first AND shows how much it saved (~X km / ~Y h) in an inline banner with a one-tap undo to restore the previous order; the reorder persists via the itinerary overlay.
features.verify_note: Independent agent: merge 99310dd on origin/main (optimizePreview/data-undo/.optundo present), test-core exits 0 with new optimizePreview guards (saves ~433km, already-shortest→0), live served checks 200 with marker optimizePreview.

features.title: Weather-smart lift-day optimizer
features.what: The Today view now protects the expensive, weather-dependent lift/summit days (Jungfraujoch, Gornergrat, Grindelwald First/Schilthorn, Innsbruck Nordkette, Seceda/Alpe di Siusi, Tre Cime). It detects a lift day via a data mark + name/tag keyword fallback, reads that lift's forecast at altitude, and either confirms a clear go-day or — if it looks socked-in (high rain %, overcast/precip code, or very cold summit max) — flags it and suggests swapping to a clearer nearby day in the trip window. Extends the B19 re-plan nudge pattern.
features.verify_note: Green-gate passed: node --check js/core.js+js/today.js, JSON.parse data/alpine.json, test-core exits 0 with 14 new liftInfo/liftScore/liftAdvice cases, local served-check 200+marker. Playwright re-drive ok:true/pageErrors:[] with in-browser eval confirming liftInfo detects Jungfraujoch(3454m), out-of-range forecast degrades to {state:pending}, and a poor forecast returns {state:poor, swap:THU 6 AUG}.

features.title: Booking countdown tracker
features.what: The Bookings view now opens with a deadline-driven 'Booking countdown' section: the flat 'bookings to make' list becomes per-item cards showing what / a derived book-by date / a live days-left countdown / booked status, sorted by sell-out risk (campsites first, then the Jungfraujoch timeslot, then time-sensitive bookings, with buy-day-of items last). Overdue and within-7-day items are flagged red. Book-by dates are derived from each item's prose lead-time relative to the trip start (2026-08-01); the countdown recomputes from the live current date every render. Ticking an item marks it booked, strikes it through, and sinks it to the bottom; the state persists in the bookings overlay.
features.verify_note: Green-gate passed: node --check js/core.js+js/bookings.js, JSON.parse data/alpine.json, test-core exits 0 with 14 new bookingCountdown cases (campsite ranks first/overdue on 2026-06-14, 'May 2026'->15 May, '1-2wk ahead'->start-10d, day-of->no deadline, booked sinks). Local served-check 200+marker. Playwright re-drive ok:true/pageErrors:[] with in-browser eval confirming 15 countdown cards render: campsites '30d overdue' (u-overdue) first, Jungfraujoch 'book by 22 Jul 2026 / 38d left' second, Gornergrat 'buy day-of' — and a toggle test flipping campsites to 'booked checkmark' sinking it to the bottom.

features.title: Offline road-pack + confirmation locker
features.what: Two ways to make the trip work with no Alpine signal. (1) The Essentials view opens with an 'Offline road-pack' card: one 'Download trip for offline' button postMessages the service worker, which refreshes every app-shell module + all trip JSON into its cache (fail-soft per asset) and reports live progress ('Caching N/total…') then a done state ('Saved for offline — N files cached'); map tiles you've panned over already persist in their own tile cache and survive untouched. (2) A confirmation locker on the 'Where you sleep' strip in Bookings: each night now has a 'conf #' field to store that pitch/lift booking reference, saved on-device in the bookings overlay (localStorage) so it shows even offline and across reloads.
features.verify_note: Green-gate passed: node --check sw.js+js/essentials.js+js/bookings.js, test-core exits 0 (incl. existing sw.js precache/tile-cache guards still green after CACHE bump v10→v11). Local served-check 200+marker; live served-check 200+marker. Playwright re-drive ok:true/pageErrors:[] with in-browser eval confirming: the offline button renders ('Download trip for offline'); clicking it runs the SW cache pass to completion ('Saved for offline — 36 files cached', cache key tc-shell-v11); 15 per-night conf fields render; a set value persists to localStorage nightConf and survives a full page reload (set 'PERSIST99' → reload → field still 'PERSIST99'). Residual console 504s are pre-existing baseline (sandbox blocks external CDN/FX; SW cross-origin fallback), present identically on pre-change main.
